body.title: [sprint] Port aider reflection loop + rich repair errors (P4)
body.description:
**Problem:** a failed apply today is a dead attempt — our generic `correction_text` ("No tool call detected...") carries zero repair information (F-C, F-E). aider converts a failed apply into a TARGETED one-shot repair via a bounded reflection loop.

**Proposed shape:** a bounded outer loop (max_reflections=3) around the P2 block-contract editor completion. On failed apply, build a rich error (port `apply_edits` error construction, editblock_coder.py:79-124): per-block failure report, 'did you mean' similar lines pulled from the ACTUAL file (`find_similar_lines`), 'REPLACE lines already present' detection, and a partial-success ledger ('the other N blocks applied — don't re-send them'). Generalizes the `_is_substantive_plan` salvage into a principled mechanism.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/architect_editor.py (the P2 editor path); error-builder ports with the P3 apply module.
**Design rules:** cap=3 is the loop's cost meter — do not raise; log every (failure-class -> successful-repair) pair to the io_corpus with role/turn labels (recurring repair shapes are future nexus rows). Repair generation stays LLM (cold path); the error BUILDER is deterministic.
**Scope boundary:** the reflection loop + rich repair-error builder + corpus logging of repair pairs. Gated on P2 existing.
**Test plan:** red->green — a first-attempt block mismatch produces a repair message CONTAINING the actual similar lines from the file (not a generic string); a second attempt with the corrected block applies; the reflection cap is enforced at 3; a (failure-class->repair) pair is written to the corpus.
**Completion criteria:** bounded reflection loop with rich, file-grounded repair errors; cap enforced; repair pairs logged to corpus with role/turn labels.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
